articles.description:
Sonic looks to reinvigorate its coffee offerings with a limited-time test of its new cold coffee lineup at participating locations in Denver, CO; Grand Junction, CO; Joplin, MO; Las Vegas, NV; New York, NY; and Monroe, LA.

The new lineup includes:
- Iced Coffee - "Smooth, sweetened coffee poured over Sonic ice."
- Cold Foam Iced Coffee - Iced coffee topped with cold foam.
- Iced Coffee Latte - Iced coffee mixed with creamer and sugar. 
- Coffee Chiller - Vanilla soft serve blended with coffee, topped with whipped topping.
The new coffee beverages are available in 16-oz and 20-oz sizes. Prices start at $2.49. Each drink is available in four varieties: Original, Caramel, Vanilla, and Oreo.
The Caramel and Vanilla varieties of each drink add caramel syrup and vanilla syrup, respectively, but the Oreo version of each drinks varies a little: the Oreo Iced Coffee adds Oreo syrup; the Oreo Iced Coffee with Cold Foam adds Oreo syrup and Oreo cookie crumbles on top; the Oreo Iced Coffee Latte adds Oreo syrup and comes crowned with whipped topping and Oreo crumbles; and the Oreo Coffee Chiller blends in Oreo syrup and Oreo crumbles and tops it all with whipped topping and Oreo crumbles.
You can check the Sonic app or website to see if the test beverages are available near you.
Photo via Sonic.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Sonic Tests New Cold Coffee Lineup

articles.description:
White Castle is testing new Cheddar Smoked Sausage Sliders and Pretzel Nibblers for a limited time in Cincinnati, Louisville, and New Jersey, to see if they merit a turn on the chain's national menu.

The Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider features a split smoked sausage link infused with cheddar cheese, served with caramelized onions on a slider bun.
New Pretzel Nibblers are fried, soft pretzel nuggets tossed in kosher salt and served with a warm and creamy cheese sauce.
Prices vary, but a Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider costs $1.29 at a White Castle restaurant in Louisville, while a 6-piece order of Pretzel Nibblers are $2.99.
Photo via White Castle.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: White Castle Tests New Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider and Pretzel Nibblers

articles.description:
Jersey Mike's is testing its new Mike's Hot Italian sub sandwich for a limited time at select locations in four markets.

The new sub features ham, salami & pepperoni lightly grilled with melted provolone cheese, served with grilled onions, lettuce, tomato, and Hot Chopped Pepper Relish on your choice of bread.
Mike's Hot Italian is a hot and spicy twist on the chain's Original Italian sandwich. It is being tested in the areas around Minneapolis, Salt Lake City, San Diego, and Chicago
A regular Mike's Hot Italian sandwich costs $11.95 in San Diego.
Photo via Jersey Mike's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jersey Mike's Tests New Hot Italian Sub

articles.description:
Crumbl welcomes the new Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup as part of this week's limited-time menu available from December 8 through December 13, 2025.

Here's what this week's national lineup at Crumbl looks like:
- New Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup - Layers of cocoa mousse, cookies & cream streusel, and dark chocolate cake, crowned with marshmallow mousse, toasted marshmallows, a chocolate drizzle, and cookies & cream pieces around the rim.
- New Holiday M&M's Cookie - A warm cookie packed with a holiday blend of M&M's Milk Chocolate Candies.
- Turtle Cheesecake - A cheesecake on a cookies & cream crust, topped with ganache, caramel, and crunchy pecan pieces.
- Holiday Birthday Cake Cookie - A warm cake batter cookie topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting and festive sprinkles.
- S'mores Brownie Cookie - A warm cookie mix of brownie, graham cracker, and milk chocolate chips, topped with and melted marshmallows.
- Molten Lava Cookie - A warm, dark chocolate cookie with a center of hot fudge, dusted with powdered sugar.
- Semi-sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and flaky sea salt.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Debuts New Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup

articles.description:
Shake Shack's Big Shack is a limited-time, Big Mac-like burger that features two quarter-pound Angus beef patties, secret sauce, green leaf lettuce, two slices of American cheese, Roma tomatoes, thick-cut pickles, and onions, on three buttered and toasted potato buns.

The Big Shack normally costs $9.99, but I used the chain's current deal, which lets you get one for free when you spend $10 or more. I bought a Coffee Shake and onion rings for a little over $10.




My particular Big Shack came with an almost comically small top bun and threatened to topple over due to its height and the very thick, lightly-pickled cucumbers and other produce.



It looked pretty good. I had a bit of a debate with myself over whether to try to cut it in half for a cross-section shot. I managed, though:



The "secret sauce" was just Shack Sauce. Compared to Mac Sauce, it's more subtle (less tangy, less sweet) and savory.
The middle bun was pointless. It might have been useful if it had been between the pickles and tomatoes to help hold them in place. The melted cheese would have been plenty enough to hold the patties. The lone leaf of lettuce didn't do much for me.
The beef patties were cooked really well. They had crispy edges, a nice sear on both sides, and a flavorful, juicy interior.



The onion rings I got on the side were excellent. They had a nice, crispy beer-battered shell and were cooked through for an easy, clean bite.



At the end of the day, I really enjoyed the Big Shack. It's basically a Double ShackBurger with a middle bun, onions, and pickles at a slight discount (much more so with the deal). While it's somewhat unwieldy to eat due to its height and construction, the tastes and textures were great, and the onion and pickles added a nice crunch, accompanied by light flavors.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Shake Shack - Big Shack

articles.description:
The Eggnog Latte and Chestnut Praline are back at Starbucks as late additions to this year's holiday menu.

The Eggnog Latte features an updated recipe that's meant to result in a "nostalgic, creamy indulgence that feels like a hug in a cup." It consists of steamed eggnog and Starbucks signature espresso, topped with creamy foam and a dusting of ground nutmeg. New this year is an iced version and an Eggnog Cream Cold Foam, which can be added to any iced coffee beverage.
The Chestnut Praline features steamed milk and espresso with a caramelized chestnut-flavored syrup, topped with whipped cream and a sprinkle of chestnut praline topping. It is available hot or iced. Chestnut Praline Cold Foam and Chestnut Praline Protein Cold Foam are new this year and available to add to any iced coffee beverage.
The new drinks and cold foams are available now for a limited time.
Photo via Starbucks.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Eggnog Latte and Chestnut Praline are Back at Starbucks

articles.description:
Taco Bell's Steak & Poblano Rolled Quesadilla features grilled marinated steak, a three-cheese blend, new roasted poblano peppers, and Spicy Ranch, rolled up in a warm, flour tortilla.

It's available for $5.69 or as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box. I went with the box for the value.
While Taco Bell calls the Steak & Poblano Rolled Quesadilla a "reimagining" of a classic quesadilla, it's more like a large (for Taco Bell) steak-and-cheese grilled burrito.
The new roasted poblano peppers held their own against the steak and cheese. While generally mild, a few bites were surprisingly spicy. They were cooked through but still retained some texture.
Without the normal filler of rice, chips, and/or beans, the Rolled Quesadilla made for one of the meatiest and cheesiest Taco Bell burritos I can remember eating. The steak was tender and flavorful, and the cheese was nicely melted.



The Spicy Ranch added a little bit of heat and some acidity.
While grilling the tortilla only retains some texture if you eat it on site, it adds a light toasty note.
Overall, Taco Bell's Steak & Poblano Rolled Quesadilla turned out really well. I really enjoyed the poblanos and the general meat-and-cheese quality of the burrito... er, rolled quesadilla.
Nutritional Info - Taco Bell Steak & Poblano Rolled Quesadilla
Calories - 530
Fat - 28g (Saturated Fat - 14g)
Sodium - 1280mg
Carbs - 43g (Sugar - 5g)
Protein - 26g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Taco Bell - Steak & Poblano Rolled Quesadilla

articles.description:
Baskin-Robbins brings back Peppermint ice cream as its Flavor of the Month for December 2025. The seasonal flavor is joined by the new Peppermint Fudge Cappy, Chocolate Fudge Cappy, and new Peppermint Avalanche Cake in a Box as Christmas fast approaches.

Additionally, the ice cream shop chain will be celebrating its 80th birthday on December 7, 2025, by offering 80-cent scoops of ice cream to Baskin-Robbins Rewards members at participating locations nationwide.
The new Peppermint Fudge Cappy features Peppermint ice cream blended with coffee and fudge, topped with whipped cream, fudge, and peppermint candy pieces. 
The Chocolate Fudge Cappy consists of Chocolate ice cream blended with coffee and fudge, topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of fudge. 
The Peppermint Avalanche Cake in a Box features layers of chocolate cake, Peppermint ice cream, Oreo cookie crumbs, peppermint crunch candy bits, whipped frosting, and fudge.
To round out the year, Baskin-Robbins Rewards members can get 31% off all scoops of ice cream on December 31, 2025, at participating locations.
Photo via Baskin-Robbins.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Baskin-Robbins Brings Back Peppermint Ice Cream

articles.description:
For a limited time, Dairy Queen is offering the Crispy Onion Cheeseburger Meal Deal for $7 at participating locations.

The meal includes a new Crispy Onion Cheeseburger, French fries, a 21-oz drink, and a small sundae.
The new burger features a seasoned beef patty, crispy onion strings, melted sharp American cheese, ketchup, mayo, and pickles, served on a toasted bun.

Mexican Pizza Empanadas are also featured in a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box. articles.description:
In celebration of National Comfort Food Day and the 2025 holiday season, Panda Express has partnered with luxury chocolatier Compartés to release a limited-edition collection of chocolate bars inspired by the chain's signature dishes.

As a promotion for the release, on December 5, 2025 (National Comfort Food Day), you can get a free Panda Express x Compartés Fortune Cookie Chocolate Bar with the purchase of any 2-item Panda Express Plate at participating locations, while supplies last. You can find a list of participating locations on the Panda Express website here. According to the terms, the freebie is "extremely limited."
The Panda Express x Compartés chocolate bars include: 
- The Original Orange Bar - Creamy milk chocolate with zesty orange bits, a hint of red chili flakes, and crispy rice for crunch.
- Honey Walnut Bar - White chocolate with candied walnuts, crisp honeycomb, and a touch of honey.
- Fortune Cookie Bar - White chocolate with crushed Panda Express fortune cookies. There's a special fortune inside.
- Kung Pao Bar - Dark chocolate with dried chili peppers, peanut bites, and flaky salt.
In addition to the "free with purchase" promotion, all four chocolate bars are available for purchase from the Compartés online store for a limited time, while supplies last. They cost $11.95 each. A special gift box set that includes all four bars in special packaging is being sold for $49.95.
Photo via Panda Express.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Panda Express Launches New Chocolate Bars Inspired by Its Dishes

articles.description:
Shake Shack is offering a series of deals throughout December, starting with a free Big Shack with a purchase of $10 or more through December 7, 2025.

The Big Shack (pictured) is the chain's latest limited-time burger. It's a riff on a Big Mac that features two quarter-pound Angus beef patties, secret sauce, green leaf lettuce, two slices of American cheese, Roma tomatoes, thick-cut pickles, and onions, on three buttered and toasted potato buns for $9.99 (which makes the deal essentially a BOGO).
The free Big Shack deal is redeemable when placing an order, using the code "SHACKCHEER", at an in-restaurant kiosk, on the Shake Shack website, or on the Shake Shack mobile app.
Other "free menu item with a $10 minimum purchase" deals coming up this month include:


12/8/25 to 12/14/25 - Free SmokeShack burger.
12/15/25 to 12/20/25 - Free Chicken Shack sandwich.
12/21/25 to 12/31/25 - Free ShackBurger.


The same code applies to all the deals.
You can find other deals at Shake Shack on the "deals & specials" page.
Photo via Shake Shack.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Shake Shack Offers Free Big Shack with $10 Purchase Through December 7, 2025 and More
